shower.thought: We spend the first years of life learning to walk and talk, then the rest of it trying to sit still and shut up.
The most honest conversations you have are probably with people you'll never see again.
You've been dead before—you just don't remember the 13.8 billion years before you were born.
Mirrors show you what you look like, but they can't show you what your face looks like when you're not thinking about your face.
Every song ever written is just someone trying to describe a feeling they couldn't quite put into words, and somehow that's more honest than actually putting it into words.
You'll never know if the color you call "red" is the same red that exists in someone else's mind.
The scariest word in any language is "sorry" because it's the only one that admits you were wrong.
We call it "falling" asleep, but nobody talks about "falling" awake—maybe one's more accurate than we'd like to admit.
Time only moves forward, which means you're always saying goodbye to moments you didn't realize were important.
You can tell everything about someone by what they do when they think no one's watching, but you can never be the one watching yourself.
If your life is a book, you don't get to know the ending, which means you're reading blind and making all your choices without the information that would actually matter.
Nobody has ever truly relaxed because the moment you realize you're relaxed, you stop being relaxed.
We're all just walking around with incomplete information about everything, pretending we know what we're doing.
The only time you see your own face without thinking about it is in a mirror, and a mirror is literally the opposite of reality.
Boredom might not be the absence of interesting things—it might be the presence of too many uninteresting things pretending to be interesting.
You'll forget most of what you learn, which means education isn't about remembering—it's about becoming someone who thinks differently.
The saddest part of aging isn't wrinkles or health—it's realizing that most people you knew are living lives you'll never hear about.
Every decision you make eliminates infinite versions of yourself that could have existed if you'd chosen differently.
We trust our memories more than we should, even though we've all experienced remembering things that definitely didn't happen that way.
When you say "I don't know," you're actually more honest than when you say "I know"—because knowing something requires certainty you probably don't actually have.

motivational.thought: The only person you're destined to become is the one you decide to be. — Ralph Waldo Emerson
Your future self will thank you for the discomfort you embrace today. — Unknown
Momentum is built one small decision at a time, not one giant leap. — Unknown
The person who says it cannot be done should not interrupt the person doing it. — Chinese Proverb
You miss 100% of the shots you don't take, but you also miss 100% of the growth that comes from trying. — Unknown
Discipline is remembering what you want more than you want comfort. — Unknown
The only real failure is the one you never learn from. — Unknown
What separates the willing from the exceptional is what they do when no one is watching. — Unknown
Your life is not measured in years, but in the moments you actually felt alive. — Unknown
Uncertainty is not a reason to wait—it's a reason to start. — Unknown
The cost of staying the same will eventually exceed the cost of change. — Unknown
Excellence is not a destination; it's a direction you choose every single day. — Unknown
Fear is just information that you're about to do something that matters. — Unknown
You are not behind. You are not late. You are exactly where you need to be to become who you're meant to be. — Unknown
Greatness is not found in the absence of struggle—it's found in how you move through it. — Unknown
The best investment you'll ever make is in yourself. — Unknown
Your potential is infinite, but your time is not. Choose wisely. — Unknown
The difference between who you are and who you want to be is what you do. — Unknown
Obstacles are not walls—they're proof that you're on a path worth taking. — Unknown
Every expert was once a beginner who refused to quit. — Unknown

darkjoke.thought: My therapist asked why I never finish anything. I said, "Well, let me tell you about my suicide att—"
I'm reading a book on the history of glue. Can't put it down.
My dad said, "Son, I'm proud of you." Then I woke up.
The worst part about having Alzheimer's is... the worst part about having Alzheimer's is...
I told my friend he was going to die alone. He said, "At least I'll have some peace and quiet."
My life is like a horror movie, except the killer is just really lazy.
I went to a grief support group. Turns out we were all there because the coffee was free.
My doctor said I needed to cut back on drinking. I said, "Doc, I'm not even sober enough to understand what you just said."
People keep telling me I'm a pessimist. I think they're right, and I hate them for it.
I asked my mom why she never wanted kids. She said, "Honey, you're the reason."
My GPS told me to take a left off the bridge. I'm starting to think it's smarter than me.
I joined a cult to make friends. Now I have friends and no way to leave. Best decision ever.
My therapist said I was being too hard on myself. I said, "You charge $200 an hour; you're not one to talk."
I told my wife I was thinking about becoming a chef. She said, "That explains the smoke detector."
The hardest part about being an organ donor is that they never call to say thank you.
My friend said, "I'm going to kill myself." I said, "Cool, can you do it when you're not around me?"
I have a stepladder because my real ladder left when I was just a kid.
I went to a restaurant that serves breakfast at any time. So I ordered French toast at 3 AM. They said, "That's not how this works." Turns out I had a misunderstanding about everything.
They say laughter is the best medicine. Tell that to my kid with leukemia.
My Fitbit won't stop telling me I need to move more. Finally, some honesty about my life choices.
